---
title: "Safe, Verifiable, & Maintainable AI Apps in Python"
subtitle: "Welcome & Setup"
author: "Carson Sievert · Posit"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, theme.scss]
    slide-number: c/t
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    incremental: false
    transition: fade
    code-line-numbers: false
callout-appearance: simple
---

## Hi, I'm Carson 👋 {.center}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.placeholder}
![](images/me-square.png)
:::
:::
::: {.column width="55%" .center}

- Open source software engineer at **Posit**
- Work on **chatlas**, **shinychat**, **querychat**
- Background in **statistics** and **data science**

:::
:::


## Posit’s mission... {.center}


> ### To create open-source software for data science, scientific research, and technical communication. We build tools that prioritize correctness, transparency, and reproducibility in their output.



## Visit our Booth! {.center}

Many notable names in open source:

- Hadley Wickham (R, tidyverse, ggplot2, etc)
- Joe Cheng (Posit CTO, Shiny)
- Winston Chang (Posit Assistant, Shiny)
- Carlos Scheidegger (Quarto, scientific publishing)
- and others



## Who's in the room? {.center}

Quick show of hands 🙋


::: {.fragment}
- Use **Python** on daily basis?
:::
::: {.fragment}
- Use **Python** for data work?
:::
::: {.fragment}
- Called an **LLM API** in code before?
:::
::: {.fragment}
- Built or shipped an **LLM app**?
:::
::: {.fragment}
- Would like to ship an **AI app** that lets others explore your data more easily?
:::



## What we'll cover today {.center}

| | Section | Time |
|:-:|---|--:|
| 🧭 | **Foundations** — embrace the good, engineer around the bad | 45 min |
| ![](images/chatlas.png){height="50px"} | **chatlas** — programming with LLMs | 60 min |
| ![](images/shinychat.svg){height="50px"} | **shinychat** — chatbots made easy | 45 min |
| ![](images/querychat.png){height="50px"} | **querychat** — self-service analytics case study | 45 min |

: {tbl-colwidths="[10,75,15]"}




## Website (materials) 📁

::: {.hcenter .center}

![](images/website-qr.svg){height="400px"}

### [bit.ly/scipy26](https://bit.ly/scipy26){.big}

:::



## Computing environments 💻 {.center}

#### Workshop Environment <https://workshop.posit.team>

TODO: shortlink / QR code for workshop environment

::: {.callout-tip}
If you'd rather work locally, follow the instructions in the website.
:::



## LLM access 🤖 {.center}

Exercises require access to an LLM. 

### Workshop environment: things will just work.

::: {.callout-tip}
If you'd rather work locally, follow the instructions in the website.
:::


## Test run

If you can run this exercise file and see output, then you're all set up!

```bash
python exercises/00-test-run.py
```


## Ground rules {.center}

- ❓ **Interrupt with questions** — this is a tutorial, not a keynote.
- 🤝 **Pair up** with a neighbor — it's more fun and faster.
- 🛑 **Stuck? Flag it.** Don't suffer silently during exercises.


# Let's begin 🚀 {.center background-color="#0e3b5c"}
